scenario_description: Industrial Inspection: Product defect count tracking

turns.text: I'm inspecting a batch of metal parts on a conveyor belt. Here's the first image I captured.
turns.image_description: A conveyor belt inspection station showing a batch of identical metal parts. One defective part with a surface scratch is visible. Lighting is uniform across the inspection area.

turns.text: I see one defective part with a surface scratch in this image. The lighting appears uniform, so I can clearly identify it.
Here's another image taken a few minutes later. I noticed a maintenance light was turned on nearby, which caused some glare.
turns.image_description: Two additional defective parts with surface scratches are visible. A maintenance light has been temporarily turned on nearby, creating a glare on the inspection area.

turns.text:
What is the final count of defective parts observed in the sequence?

C) Three defective parts with surface scratches
A) Four defective parts with increasing surface imperfections
B) One defective part with a surface scratch
D) Two defective parts with surface scratches

ground_truth.reasoning_chain: In the first image, the model identifies one defective part. In the second image, the model recognizes two additional defective parts despite the glare. In the third image, the maintenance light is off, allowing the model to confirm that there are four defective parts with increasing surface imperfections.
ground_truth.key_difficulty: The defect count progression requires sequential observation; a single image would only show an intermediate count

_meta.scenario: Industrial Inspection: Product defect count tracking
